title: The Checklist Manifesto
subtitle: How to Get Things Right
author: Atul Gawande
publisher: Metropolitan Books / Henry Holt and Company
themes: How checklists extend the power of the human mind under complexity
The danger of professional overconfidence and expert error
Simplicity as the primary weapon against overwhelming complexity
The discipline-dignity connection in high-stakes work
Communication and coordination as checklist byproducts
Humility and respect for the limits of individual memory
Why experts resist and then must adopt formalized safeguards
awards: One of The Economist's Best Books (2009)
excerpt: In a world of mounting complexity, the most powerful tool for getting things right is already in your hands: a simple, disciplined checklist. Atul Gawande reveals how a surgeon, a building contractor, and a NASA engineer share the same secret strategy for managing the impossible.
